seo.title: LTX 2.3 Pro | AI Video Model, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use LTX 2.3 Pro on MaxVideoAI for higher-fidelity video generation, audio-led workflows, Extend, and Retake, with live specs and pricing before you render.

overview: LTX 2.3 Pro is the current LTX model on MaxVideoAI for higher-fidelity video generation, audio-led workflows, Extend, and Retake.

hero.title: LTX 2.3 Pro — current LTX model for audio, extend and retake workflows
hero.intro: LTX 2.3 Pro is the current LTX model for higher-fidelity video generation, audio-led workflows, Extend, and Retake when you need the main current LTX path for polished production work.

secondaryLinks.label: Compare LTX 2.3 Fast vs LTX 2.3 Pro

technicalOverview.label: Generate durations
technicalOverview.body: 6 s, 8 s or 10 s for Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video

technicalOverview.label: Frame rate
technicalOverview.body: 24, 25, 48 or 50 fps for Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video

technicalOverview.label: Inputs by mode
technicalOverview.body: Text prompt, start image plus optional end image, uploaded audio, or source video depending on the selected workflow

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio toggle for generate modes, plus a dedicated Audio-to-Video workflow driven by an uploaded audio file

technicalOverview.label: Editing workflows
technicalOverview.body: Extend at the start or end with context controls; retake a selected time window and replace audio, video, or both

technicalOverview.label: Pricing
technicalOverview.body: Pay-as-you-go; displayed generate pricing starts around $0.08/s at 1080p, $0.16/s at 1440p, and $0.31/s at 4K

promptStructure.title: How to prompt LTX 2.3 Pro by workflow
promptStructure.quote: Mode: Generate Video. Subject: [who/what]. Action: [single beat]. Camera: [move]. Look: [lighting + color]. Format: [16:9 or 9:16, duration, resolution]. Audio: [ambience, dialogue or music cue].
promptStructure.description: Treat LTX 2.3 Pro like a family of workflows, not one single prompt box. The prompt should match the mode: generation, audio-driven animation, extension, or retake.
steps: For Generate Video, describe subject, action, camera, look, duration and format.
For Image-to-Video, use the prompt to define motion while the start image anchors the first frame.
For start-to-end transitions, pair a start image with an optional end image and describe how the scene evolves between them.
For Audio-to-Video, let the uploaded audio define timing and use the prompt only to steer scene intent, energy and camera motion.
For Extend, describe what should happen before or after the existing source video.
For Retake, explain exactly what should change in the selected section instead of rewriting the full clip.

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Use Audio-to-Video when timing, lip motion, or musical phrasing should follow a real soundtrack.
Use start and end images only when the transition target is clear; otherwise keep Image-to-Video on a single start frame.
Use Extend when the shot is already good and you only need more lead-in or tail-out.
Use Retake when one section is wrong but the rest of the clip should stay intact.
Keep 4K for final passes; iterate at lower cost first, then upscale your winner workflow.

prompts.title: Text-to-Video cinematic ad beat
prompts.prompt: A premium sneaker launch film in 16:9, dramatic side light, slow tracking move across the product, light mist in the air, subtle reflections on the floor, 8 seconds, polished brand-audio bed.

prompts.title: Image-to-Video with end frame
prompts.prompt: Start from the uploaded still of a fashion portrait, introduce a slow handheld push-in and soft hair movement, then resolve toward the uploaded end frame as neon reflections intensify.

prompts.title: Audio-to-Video performance setup
prompts.prompt: Use the uploaded audio as the timing backbone for a moody stage performance, with restrained camera movement, strong backlight, and emotional close-ups that rise with the chorus.
prompts.notes: Upload the audio first; optional start image helps lock the opening frame.

faqs.q: What is new in LTX 2.3 Pro compared with the older LTX 2.0 pages?
faqs.a: LTX 2.3 Pro adds a unified workflow surface around generate modes plus Audio-to-Video, Extend and Retake, while also supporting 16:9 and 9:16 in the generate flows.

faqs.q: What is the difference between Extend and Retake?
faqs.a: Extend continues a source video before or after the existing footage. Retake replaces a selected section inside the source video, with controls for start time, duration and replacement mode.

faqs.q: Does LTX 2.3 Pro support native audio?
faqs.a: Yes. Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video expose a native audio toggle, and Audio-to-Video uses an uploaded audio file as the driving input.

faqs.q: Does LTX 2.3 Fast include Audio-to-Video, Extend or Retake?
faqs.a: No. LTX 2.3 Fast is limited to text-to-video and image-to-video in the current public Fal routing.

custom.heroDesc1: LTX 2.3 Pro is the most workflow-complete LTX page in MaxVideoAI. It covers generation, audio-driven animation, source-video extension, and selective retakes from one engine instead of splitting those tasks across separate model pages.
custom.heroDesc2: That matters for production: you can block a shot from text, refine it from an image, test an audio-led version, extend the winning take, then retake only the broken section without changing engines.
custom.heroSupportLine: Best for teams that want one LTX surface for ideation, sound-led animation, and quick editorial fixes.
custom.whyTitle: Why LTX 2.3 Pro is strategically useful
heroHighlights: One engine, multiple workflows||Generate, animate from sound, extend existing clips, and retake broken sections without leaving the LTX family.
Audio is no longer just a toggle||You can either generate native audio in standard runs or drive the clip from an uploaded soundtrack in Audio-to-Video.
Better editing leverage||Extend and Retake are practical tools for keeping a good shot instead of starting from zero.
Fits modern content formats||Landscape and vertical generate modes make it easier to plan both hero and social variants.
custom.specTitle: Real Specs - LTX 2.3 Pro in MaxVideoAI
custom.specNote: These specs describe the live public Fal routing exposed in MaxVideoAI today, not a theoretical future feature list.

specSections.title: Generate workflows
specSections.intro: Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video are the broadest modes: 6 to 10 seconds, 1080p to 4K, vertical or landscape, with native audio available.
items: Use Text-to-Video for net-new story beats.
Use Image-to-Video when the first frame must stay on-brand.
Add an end image only when the final composition matters.
Pick 9:16 when the clip is designed for short-form social.

specSections.title: Audio, extend and retake
specSections.intro: The biggest difference versus simpler engines is not just image quality. It is that LTX 2.3 Pro covers timing-led animation and source-clip edits from the same page.
items: Audio-to-Video turns uploaded audio into the timing spine of the clip.
Extend can grow a strong shot at the head or tail.
Retake is useful when only one section needs replacement.
Context controls help preserve continuity during extension.

custom.whatTitle: How LTX 2.3 Pro works in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Choose LTX 2.3 Pro, then stay in Generate Video for standard text/image flows or switch to Extend Video / Retake Video when you need source-video editing. Audio-to-Video is selected automatically when an audio source is present.
custom.whatIntro2: This structure keeps the page focused on one engine while still exposing the real Fal workflows behind it.
whatFlowSteps: Pick LTX 2.3 Pro as the engine.
Stay on Generate Video for text, image and audio-led generation, or activate Extend Video / Retake Video when needed.
Choose 6, 8 or 10 seconds for text and image generation, plus format, resolution and fps.
Upload the relevant asset for the active workflow: start image, end image, audio source or source video.
Review the live price before generation, then iterate only on the workflow that matters.
custom.galleryIntro: Representative LTX 2.3 Pro examples and placeholders used on this branch for layout and prompt coverage.
strengths: Most complete LTX workflow surface: generate, audio-driven motion, extension and retake in one place.
Useful for vertical and landscape planning when the same concept must ship to multiple placements.
Extend and Retake reduce waste because you can preserve the strong parts of a clip.
boundaries: 4K, 9:16 and fps controls currently apply to the standard generate modes, not every editing workflow.
Audio-to-Video, Extend and Retake are standard LTX 2.3 Pro only, not Fast.
Retake still needs a precise source window and a clear prompt to avoid drifting too far from the original clip.
safetyRules: Do not generate sexual content or anything involving minors.
Do not impersonate real people or public figures without clear authorization.
Do not upload private or sensitive personal data in source media.
Keep dialogue, likeness and music usage rights clear when using Audio-to-Video or Retake.
demoPrompt: Use the uploaded song preview as timing reference for a moody warehouse dance performance, with slow lateral camera movement in the intro and tighter emotional close-ups when the chorus lands.
demoNotes: Prompt adds staging and camera direction
Optional opening image can lock the first frame
custom.multishotIntro1: LTX 2.3 Pro is less about long-form generation than about keeping momentum when a shot already exists.
custom.multishotIntro2: Use Extend for continuation and Retake for repairs instead of rebuilding a sequence from scratch.
multishotTips: Extend when the ending is too abrupt but the shot is otherwise strong.
Retake when one line, gesture, or motion beat fails inside a usable clip.
custom.imageIntro: The start image is the opening anchor for Image-to-Video, and an optional end image can define where the motion should resolve.
imageFlow: Upload the start image that should act as the first frame.
Optionally upload an end image when the final composition matters.
Use the prompt for camera motion, environmental change and pacing between those two points.
imageWhy: Useful for on-brand portrait and product transitions
Helps bridge still campaigns into motion deliverables
comparisonPoints: Choose LTX 2.3 Pro when you need more than standard generation: audio-driven motion, continuation, or selective retakes.
Use it when a source clip already exists and you want to extend or repair instead of regenerating everything.
Choose LTX 2.3 Fast only when speed and simple generate workflows matter more than editing controls.
custom.comparisonCta: Compare LTX 2.3 Pro vs LTX 2.3 Fast ->
custom.relatedSubtitle: Compare LTX 2.3 Pro against faster draft models and premium cinematic engines without leaving the same cluster.
custom.finalPara1: Ready to test a full LTX workflow with audio, extend and retake in one place? Open LTX 2.3 Pro in Generate.
custom.finalPara2: Use the standard model when the project needs more than just a fast first pass.
custom.promptingTitle: How to Prompt LTX 2.3 Pro by Workflow
custom.promptingIntro: LTX 2.3 Pro changes shape depending on the job. Prompt fresh generation, audio-led animation, extend, and retake differently instead of using one universal template.
custom.promptingTip: Tip: duration, resolution, fps, and workflow controls already live in the UI. Use the prompt to steer the active route: new shot, soundtrack-led motion, continuation, or surgical fix.
promptingGlobalPrinciples: Define one subject, one core beat and one camera move before layering style.
Use the workflow that matches the job: generate, audio, extend or retake.
For retakes, describe only the section that must change instead of rewriting the full video.
promptingEngineWhy: Text and image generation stay direct and cinematic.
Audio-to-Video works best when the soundtrack carries timing and the prompt steers staging.
Extend and Retake are strongest when the prompt is specific about continuity and what must remain unchanged.

promptingTabNotes.quick: Generate = create a new shot from text or image.
promptingTabNotes.structured: Audio = let the soundtrack carry timing and pacing.
promptingTabNotes.pro: Extend = add the next beat without drifting.
promptingTabNotes.storyboard: Retake = change only the damaged or weak section.

promptingTabs.label: Generate
promptingTabs.title: Generate prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use when LTX is creating a fresh shot from text or a start image.
promptingTabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what appears]

Action:
[One visible beat]

Camera:
[Shot + one move]

Look:
[Lighting + palette + texture]

Use this when the scene starts from scratch.

promptingTabs.label: Audio
promptingTabs.title: Audio-to-video prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use when soundtrack or dialogue should drive timing and staging.
promptingTabs.copy:
Audio source:
[What the soundtrack / dialogue / beat is doing]

Visual staging:
[What the viewer should see while the audio plays]

Camera:
[How the shot follows the rhythm]

Rule:
Let the audio set pacing. Use the prompt to control subject, staging, and camera.

promptingTabs.label: Extend
promptingTabs.title: Extend prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use when the current clip is good and you only need the next beat.
promptingTabs.copy:
Current source:
[What already exists]

Next beat:
[What should happen after the existing clip]

Continuity to preserve:
[Identity / setting / camera rhythm / light]

Rule:
Describe only the continuation. Do not rewrite the whole clip.

promptingTabs.label: Retake
promptingTabs.title: Retake prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use when only one part of the clip needs to change.
promptingTabs.copy:
Current source:
[What is already correct]

Section to retake:
[Exact moment or visual problem]

What must stay unchanged:
[Everything outside the retake window]

Replacement beat:
[What the new section should do instead]

Rule:
Retake prompts should be surgical. Ask for one repair, not a new scene.

troubleshootingItems: Audio-to-Video feels visually disconnected -> reduce the scene complexity and let the soundtrack drive the pacing.
Extend drifts too far from the original clip -> describe continuity anchors and tighten the new action.
Retake changes more than the target section -> shorten the retake window and be explicit about what must stay the same.
Image-to-Video feels generic -> make the start frame stronger and simplify the motion request.
4K runs feel expensive for ideation -> validate the motion in a cheaper pass first, then rerun the winner.
